message:
feat(btree,vdbe): lazy payload prefix read for on-demand Column decode

Add infrastructure to read only the first N bytes of a B-tree cell
payload and parse the record header from that prefix. The VDBE
Column opcode now fetches a 256-byte prefix on first access and
extends the buffer on demand when a column's byte range exceeds
what's been read. This avoids reading entire multi-KB rows when
only the first few columns are needed.

btree/cursor.rs:
- New read_cell_payload_prefix_into(): reads at most
  max_prefix_bytes from a cell, resolving overflow pages only
  as far as needed (stops early once the prefix is satisfied)
- New public payload_prefix_into() on BtreeCursorOps for BtCursor

btree/overflow.rs:
- Refactor read_overflow_chain_into as a wrapper around new
  read_overflow_chain_prefix_into(max_prefix_bytes) that stops
  copying once the prefix byte count is reached
- Size-mismatch assertion moved to the full-read wrapper

btree/traits.rs:
- Add payload_prefix_into(cx, max_prefix_bytes, buf) to the
  BtreeCursorOps trait
- Implement on MockBtreeCursor for tests

types/record.rs:
- New parse_record_header_prefix_into(): parses varint record
  header from a potentially-truncated payload prefix, returning
  column offsets even when the body is incomplete
- New RecordDecodeScratch::prepare_for_record_prefix(): initializes
  scratch from a prefix with header-only parsing
- Test: parse_record_header_prefix_accepts_truncated_body verifies
  correct column offsets from a 3-column record truncated to
  header+1 byte

vdbe/engine.rs:
- STORAGE_CURSOR_LAYOUT_PREFIX_BYTES = 256: initial prefix size
- New CursorBackend::payload_prefix_into() dispatching to all
  backend types
- New column_payload_end(): computes end byte offset from
  ColumnOffset
- New ensure_storage_cursor_payload_prefix(): fetches at least N
  bytes into cursor buffer
- New ensure_storage_cursor_row_layout(): replaces eager full-
  payload fetch with lazy scheme — fetches 256-byte prefix on
  first access, parses record header, extends buffer on subsequent
  Column accesses when column byte range exceeds what's buffered
- Column opcode handler updated to use lazy layout instead of
  full row cache, with doubling retry loop for large headers
- Existing ensure_storage_cursor_row_cache preserved for other
  call sites

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
